campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to hear an update from the regional transit authority about how their money was invested in March, and they discussed a new policy about what the city's buildings and property can be used for. The council also talked about job openings in the city and ways to keep city employees from leaving.
keyTopics: Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority's March 2026 investment report
New policy restricting use of city property for federal civil purposes
City job vacancies and employee retention strategies

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a meeting to address unsafe or abandoned properties in Saratoga that need to be cleaned up or demolished. They also adopted a new technology policy for the city and set up a process to help residents who need special accommodations for city services.
keyTopics: Public hearing on removing hazardous properties identified by the County
New Technology Disruption Policy to replace outdated tech guidelines
Process for residents to request reasonable accommodations from the city

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — May 5, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met to handle routine business including approving previous meeting notes and dealing with fire safety concerns. The council declared overgrown brush and vegetation as a public hazard and approved service agreements for landscape maintenance and lighting in the area.
keyTopics: Hazardous vegetation (overgrown brush) declared a public nuisance - likely related to fire prevention
Approved landscape and lighting maintenance engineer's report
Authorized multi-year service contracts with Pacific (vendor name incomplete in agenda)

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 6, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to plan upcoming agendas for May 12 and May 19, 2026. They reviewed what items should be added to these meetings, set specific times for certain discussions, and checked whether proper notice requirements were met. The council also received a monthly activity report from the City Auditor covering April 2026.
keyTopics: Planning the May 12, 2026 council meeting agenda
Planning the May 19, 2026 council meeting agenda
Setting specific times for scheduled agenda items
Reviewing public notice requirements for agenda items
City Auditor's April 2026 activity report

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on May 7, 2026 that included both in-person and online participation options. Residents were able to join the discussion virtually via Zoom or phone to comment on agenda items.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access available via Zoom and phone
Public comment procedures for remote participants
Meeting conducted with hybrid in-person and online component

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 5, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: Sunnyvale's City Council held a special closed-door meeting at 5 P.M. to discuss confidential matters, followed by a regular public council meeting at 7 P.M. The agenda focused on supporting small business in the community. The specific details of what was discussed in the closed session were not made public.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting (details not disclosed to public)
Small business support initiatives
Regular council meeting convened at 7 P.M.
No specific public agenda items detailed in this document

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 11, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held an initial meeting to review and discuss the proposed budget for fiscal year 2026-27. The council also covered requirements for lobbyists—people who try to influence city decisions—to register and report their activities to the city.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget proposal review
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements
City of Cupertino Municipal Code Chapter 2.100 compliance

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 11, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's City Council held a meeting on May 11, 2026 focused on economic development and marketing initiatives for the city. The council is accepting both in-person attendance and remote participation via Zoom or phone for residents who want to get involved or provide feedback.
keyTopics: Economic Development & Marketing Committee meeting
In-person and remote participation options available
Public comment submission deadline: 11 AM day-of via email

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve spending plans for the next several years and discuss the city's budget for 2026-27. They also finalized a master plan for new parks and trails in the Innovation District and updated their internal meeting procedures.
keyTopics: Approved 5-year capital spending plan (2027-2031)
Reviewed proposed operating budget for fiscal year 2026-27
Adopted Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan
Updated City Council meeting procedures and protocols

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 7, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The city council held a hearing about a plan to build a new 6-story apartment building with 72 units at 450 Lytton Avenue, replacing a city parking lot. All units would be affordable housing for lower-income residents, and the developer is asking for special allowances to make the project work on the small lot.
keyTopics: 72 affordable housing units replacing a 52-space parking lot
Six-story building on a very small 0.43-acre lot in downtown Palo Alto
Request for density bonus law concessions (waivers from normal building rules)
City evaluating environmental review requirements
